questionText: A spherical balloon is being inflated. When the radius is $4$ cm, the radius is increasing at $0.25$ cm/s. Find the rate at which the volume is increasing at this instant.

steps.explanation: The volume of a sphere depends on its radius, so we start with the standard formula linking $V$ and $r$.
Differentiate both sides with respect to $t$ using the chain rule: the derivative of $r^{3}$ brings down $3r^{2}$, and we multiply by $\dfrac{dr}{dt}$ because $r$ changes with time.
Insert $r = 4$ cm and $\dfrac{dr}{dt} = 0.25$ cm/s into the connected-rates equation.
Carry out the arithmetic to obtain the rate at which the volume is changing.
Volume rate has cubic units per second.
This is how fast the volume is increasing at the instant when the radius is the given value.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: The volume is increasing at $\dfrac{dV}{dt} = 4\pi$ cm$^{3}$/s.
